# Implementation-Readiness Checklist: Parse, Chunk & Embed — RAG Index Build

**Purpose**: Validate that the requirements are complete, unambiguous, consistent, and measurable enough that an implementer (including a smaller model) can build the feature without guessing or hallucinating. This tests the **requirements**, not the implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-10
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-checklist`. Each item asks whether the spec *says enough*; an unchecked item means the spec needs a clarifying edit (or a deliberate "out of scope" note) before `/speckit-plan`.

## Requirement Completeness — Data Model & Migration

- [x] CHK001 Are the required fields of the **Chunk** entity each defined with type and nullability (text, chunk_type, section, drug, date, source_reliability, embedding, lexical vector, model version, ordering position)? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, §FR-007]
- [x] CHK002 Is the **provenance of `chunk.date`** specified (e.g., inherited from the document's published date vs. parsed from content)? [Gap, §FR-007]
- [x] CHK003 Is the **provenance of the chunk's embedder model version** specified as coming from the modelserver's per-result stamp (spec 5) rather than a locally hard-coded value? [Clarity, §FR-007, §FR-016]
- [x] CHK004 Is the stored value for **`section` when the source has no section structure** defined (null vs. a default label)? [Gap, §FR-002]
- [x] CHK005 Are the columns and cardinality of the new **`document_index_state`** table specified (1:1 with documents, the four status values, embedder version)? [Completeness, §FR-010, §Clarifications]
- [x] CHK006 Are the columns of the new **`index_build_runs`** record specified (client scope, the four counts, outcome/status, timing)? [Completeness, §FR-010, §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK007 Does the spec state whether **`document_index_state` and `index_build_runs` carry their own `client_id`** for tenant-scoped queries, or rely solely on the document/parent link? [Gap, §FR-014]
- [x] CHK008 Are the **indexes required for idempotency and isolation** specified (e.g., the not-yet-indexed lookup, client scoping) in addition to the retrieval indexes? [Completeness, §FR-009, §FR-015]
- [x] CHK009 Is the requirement that the migration is **purely additive (no ALTER of any spec-4 table)** stated unambiguously? [Clarity, §FR-021, §Clarifications]
- [x] CHK010 Are **reversible-downgrade** requirements stated for all three new tables and the pgvector extension enablement? [Completeness, §FR-021]
- [x] CHK011 Is the **embedding dimension fixed at 768** stated as a stored-schema constraint (not just a runtime check)? [Clarity, §FR-016]

## Requirement Completeness — Pipeline & Processing

- [x] CHK012 Is there a dedicated parser mapping for **each of the seven `source` values** (pubmed, europepmc, openfda_faers, openfda_label, fda_medwatch, ema, mhra), matching the spec-4 source enum? [Coverage, §FR-001]
- [x] CHK013 When a deduplicated document has **multiple `document_sources` raw payloads** (e.g., PubMed + Europe PMC), does the spec define which payload(s) are parsed — all-and-merge, or the single highest-reliability source? [Gap, §FR-009, Spec-4 dedup]
- [x] CHK014 Is the **unit of "token"** for the ~256/512 limits defined as the modelserver embedder's own tokenizer (so chunk sizing matches what the embedder will actually truncate)? [Ambiguity, §FR-008]
- [x] CHK015 Are the **overlap exemptions** for structural chunks (whole table / figure_caption) stated clearly enough to be unambiguous? [Clarity, §FR-008]
- [x] CHK016 Is the requirement to **embed every chunk type** (text, table, figure_caption, structured_data), not only prose, explicit? [Completeness, §FR-005, §FR-006]
- [x] CHK017 Is the **manual-trigger endpoint's authorization** specified (which staff role(s) may invoke it, and use of the `acting_client` scoping pattern from spec 4b)? [Gap, §FR-017]
- [x] CHK018 Is the **route shape** of the trigger consistent with spec-4b's `/clients/{client_id}/...` convention? [Consistency, §FR-017]
- [x] CHK019 Is the **embedder-version re-index campaign** (re-embedding the existing corpus after an embedder upgrade) — referenced in the §Clarifications rationale — either specified as a requirement or explicitly declared out of scope? [Conflict, §FR-022, §Clarifications]

## Requirement Clarity & Ambiguity

- [x] CHK020 Is the boundary between **`indexed-empty` and `errored`** defined with objective criteria (when does zero-chunk output mean "done, empty" vs. "failed")? [Ambiguity, §Edge Cases, §FR-010]
- [x] CHK021 Is the criterion for a document being **"parseable" vs. "unparseable"** defined precisely enough to be testable? [Clarity, §SC-001, §FR-011]
- [x] CHK022 Does the spec define whether **`errored` documents are retried on the next run or permanently skipped** (idempotency vs. resilience interaction)? [Gap, §FR-009, §FR-011, §FR-013]
- [x] CHK023 Is **"chunk ordering position"** stated as a requirement (not only mentioned in the entity description), including how order is determined? [Consistency, §Key Entities, §FR-007]
- [x] CHK024 Is the **table-serialization rule** ("headers accompany each row, never split a logical row") precise about behavior when a single row exceeds the token cap? [Ambiguity, §FR-003, §Edge Cases]

## Requirement Consistency & Conflicts

- [x] CHK025 Does requiring an **index on the `drug` column** (§FR-015) conflict with `drug` being **always null in v1** (§FR-023)? Is indexing-an-empty-column intended or should the index be deferred? [Conflict, §FR-015, §FR-023]
- [x] CHK026 Are the **idempotency guarantees** (§FR-009) and **resumability guarantees** (§FR-013) consistent about what marks a document "complete" (state transition vs. chunk existence)? [Consistency, §FR-009, §FR-013]
- [x] CHK027 Is **source_reliability inheritance** consistently stated (chunk inherits the document's single, highest tier) across FR-007 and US2 scenario 6? [Consistency, §FR-007, §US2]
- [x] CHK028 Are the **scope exclusions** (retrieval, rerank, corroboration, triage, redaction, cron) stated consistently between §FR-018, §Out of Scope, and the §Dependencies handoffs to specs 7/8/9/11/12? [Consistency, §FR-018, §Out of Scope]

## Multi-Tenant Isolation & PII (Coverage)

- [x] CHK029 Are **client-scoping requirements** stated for every new table and every read/write path (chunks, state, runs), not just chunks? [Coverage, §FR-014]
- [x] CHK030 Is the **cross-client zero-leakage** outcome stated as objectively measurable (a cross-client read returns 0 foreign chunks)? [Measurability, §SC-002, §FR-014]
- [x] CHK031 Is the **PII-free logging** requirement specific about which fields are forbidden in logs (e.g., FAERS age/sex/country) and confirmed for the parse + embed paths? [Clarity, §FR-019, §SC-007]
- [x] CHK032 Is the **redaction boundary** unambiguous — that stored chunk text is faithful-but-unredacted in this spec and active Presidio redaction is spec 12's responsibility before consumption? [Clarity, §FR-019, §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK033 Are **erasure/cascade** requirements complete for all new tables — do `document_index_state` and `index_build_runs` get purged with their document/client alongside chunks? [Coverage, §FR-020]

## Retrieval-Readiness (Coverage for Spec 7)

- [x] CHK034 Is the **lexical full-text search vector** requirement specific about what text it derives from and (where relevant) its language configuration? [Gap, §FR-015]
- [x] CHK035 Does the spec require **both** a dense embedding and a populated lexical vector on every chunk (no chunk with only one)? [Completeness, §FR-015, §SC-008]
- [x] CHK036 Are the **metadata fields spec-7 will filter/rank on** (chunk_type, section, source_reliability, document link) all required to be persisted and indexed? [Coverage, §FR-015]
- [x] CHK037 Is the **pgvector index type/strategy** decision either specified or explicitly deferred to planning, so the implementer knows it is a deliberate open choice rather than an omission? [Assumption, §FR-015]

## Acceptance Criteria & Measurability

- [x] CHK038 Is each Success Criterion **objectively measurable** without implementation knowledge (counts, 0/100% thresholds)? [Measurability, §SC-001–§SC-008]
- [x] CHK039 Is **SC-009 "within minutes"** quantified or explicitly marked as a qualitative target, so it isn't mistaken for a hard threshold? [Measurability, §SC-009]
- [x] CHK040 Does every functional requirement have at least one **acceptance scenario or success criterion** tracing to it (no orphan FRs)? [Traceability, §Requirements, §Success Criteria]

## Edge Cases, Failure & Recovery Coverage

- [x] CHK041 Are requirements defined for **concurrent index builds for the same client** (double-trigger) so duplicate chunks cannot result? [Gap, Coverage, §FR-009]
- [x] CHK042 Is the **whole-run modelserver-unavailable** path specified end-to-end (run status recorded, no partial chunk set persisted for incomplete documents, fully resumable)? [Coverage, §Edge Cases, §FR-013]
- [x] CHK043 Is **atomicity** required between writing a document's chunks and its state transition (so a crash mid-document cannot leave it "indexed" with partial chunks)? [Gap, §FR-013]
- [x] CHK044 Are the **degenerate-input** cases (document with only an identifier / empty summary, header-less or ragged table) each given a defined, non-crashing outcome? [Coverage, §Edge Cases]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK045 Are all **upstream dependencies** (spec-4 `documents` + `document_sources.raw_payload`, spec-5 `ModelserverClient`, pgvector) stated with what this spec relies on from each? [Completeness, §Dependencies]
- [x] CHK046 Is the **immutable-document assumption** (no re-embed on payload change) stated clearly and not contradicted by any FR? [Assumption, §FR-022, §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK047 Are the **deferred-to-later-spec** items (query cache → 7, NER drug tagging → 8, cron → 11, redaction/erasure-of-vectors → 12, PDF parsing → stretch) each named so the implementer does not build them prematurely? [Assumption, §Out of Scope, §Dependencies]

## Notes

- **Re-validated 2026-06-10 (post plan/research/data-model/contracts + /speckit-analyze): 47/47 PASS.** Every item is resolved by the spec or a design artifact in this package. The last two residuals were closed with spec edits: CHK019 (embedder-upgrade re-index campaign → declared Out of Scope) and CHK024 (a single structural chunk exceeding the token cap → hard-split-at-token-boundary rule added to §Edge Cases / FR-008 / chunker task T011).
- Check items off as the spec is confirmed to cover them: `[x]`. An item left unchecked is a signal to add a one-line clarifying requirement (or an explicit "out of scope" note) before planning.
- **Highest-leverage gaps for a smaller implementer model** (most likely to cause wrong guesses): CHK013 (multi-source payload selection), CHK014 (token unit), CHK017 (trigger auth), CHK022 (retry errored?), CHK025 (drug index vs null conflict), CHK041 (concurrent builds), CHK043 (chunk/state atomicity).
- Most items are traceable to a spec section; `[Gap]`/`[Ambiguity]`/`[Conflict]`/`[Assumption]` mark where the spec is silent or under-specified rather than referencing existing text.
